# Elasticsearch Cluster Upgrade & Migration Plan

**Prepared:** 2026-06-25  
**Cluster:** `elasticsearch` namespace, `es` StatefulSet  
**Review status:** DRAFT — awaiting SRE lead sign-off before any change is applied

---

## 1. Current State Inventory

### 1.1 Cluster Identity

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Cluster name | `elasticsearch` |
| Cluster UUID | `e0Y4F9R2SUiSMmJQk7WQEA` |
| Version | **8.11.1** (build 2023-11-11) |
| Lucene | 9.8.0 |
| JVM | OpenJDK 21.0.1 (bundled) |
| License | Basic (active) |
| Health | **green** |

### 1.2 Node Topology

Three nodes, all sharing every role (`cdfhilmrstw` = candidate/data/frozen/hot/ilm/master/remote-cluster-client/transform/warm):

| Pod | IP | Heap used | Disk used |
|---|---|---|---|
| es-0 | 10.244.0.33 | 48% | 37.3% |
| es-1 | 10.244.0.32 | 41% | 37.3% |
| es-2 | 10.244.0.31 | 21% | 37.3% |

**Resource allocation per pod:**

| Resource | Request | Limit |
|---|---|---|
| CPU | 500m | (none) |
| Memory | 1 Gi | 2 Gi |
| JVM heap (-Xms/-Xmx) | 512 Mi | 512 Mi |

**Storage per node:** 5 Gi PVC, `standard` StorageClass, `ReadWriteOnce`.

### 1.3 Kubernetes Infrastructure

- **StatefulSet:** `es`, replicas=3, `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`
- **Services:** `es-http` (ClusterIP :9200), `es-nodes` (headless :9200/:9300)
- **Config:** `es-config` ConfigMap mounts `elasticsearch.yml`
- **TLS certs:** `es-http-tls` Secret, CN=`es-http`, CA=`es-ca`
- **Init containers:** `sysctl` (sets `vm.max_map_count=262144`, privileged) + `fix-perms` (chown data dir)

### 1.4 Live Configuration (elasticsearch.yml)

```yaml
cluster.name: elasticsearch
node.name: ${NODE_NAME}
network.host: 0.0.0.0
discovery.seed_hosts: es-nodes.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
cluster.initial_master_nodes: es-0,es-1,es-2   # ← WARNING: see §4.1
xpack.security.enabled: "true"
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: "true"
xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: "true"
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
```

### 1.5 Cluster API Settings (persistent / transient)

```
GET /_cluster/settings?flat_settings=true
→ {"persistent":{},"transient":{}}
```

No cluster-level settings have been applied via the API. All configuration is file-based.

### 1.6 Indices

| Index | Shards | Replicas | Docs | Health |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `test-index` | 1 | 1 | 1 | green |

**`test-index` non-default settings:**

```json
"routing.allocation.include._tier_preference": "data_content"
```

This forces shard placement onto `data_content`-tier nodes. All three nodes currently carry all roles (including the `data_content` tier implicitly), so the constraint is satisfied, but it will pin shards if dedicated tier nodes are introduced later.

### 1.7 ILM Policies

All ILM policies present are Elasticsearch built-in system policies (`"_meta": {"managed": true}`). No user-defined ILM policies exist. None of the built-in policies are currently bound to any index.

Notable built-in policies in place:

| Policy | Hot rollover | Warm | Delete |
|---|---|---|---|
| `logs` | 30d / 50 GB | — | — |
| `metrics` | 30d / 50 GB | — | — |
| `7-days-default` | 7d / 50 GB | 2d (forcemerge+shrink) | 7d |
| `30-days-default` | 30d / 50 GB | 2d (forcemerge+shrink) | 30d |
| `90-days-default` | 30d / 50 GB | 2d (forcemerge) | 90d |
| `.monitoring-8-ilm-policy` | 3d / 50 GB | forcemerge | 3d |

### 1.8 Index Templates

All index templates present are Elasticsearch built-in system templates. No user-defined composable or legacy templates exist.

### 1.9 Security

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| `xpack.security.enabled` | true |
| HTTP TLS | enabled (xpack.security.http.ssl) |
| Transport TLS | enabled, `verification_mode: certificate` |
| HTTP compression | **disabled** (`http.compression: false`) |
| TLS cert subject | CN=es-http |
| TLS cert issuer | CN=es-ca (self-signed CA) |
| TLS cert validity | 2026-06-25 → **2027-06-25** |
| License | basic |

---

## 2. Upgrade Target

**Recommended target:** Elasticsearch **8.17.x** (latest stable 8.x as of the plan date).

Rationale: 8.11 → 8.17 is a same-major rolling upgrade, requiring no index re-creation or breaking API changes. A jump to 9.x would require additional preparation (review of all deprecated settings, re-index of indices created before 7.0, and a 9.x-specific migration guide check) and is deferred to a subsequent cycle.

**Minimum compatibility check:**
- `minimum_wire_compatibility_version: 7.17.0` — all nodes will remain same-version throughout the rolling upgrade, so no wire-compat issue.
- `minimum_index_compatibility_version: 7.0.0` — `test-index` was created by ES 8.5 (`version.created: 8500003`), well within range.

---

## 3. Pre-Upgrade Checklist

Before touching the StatefulSet image tag, complete all items in this section.

### 3.1 Snapshot / Backup

1. Register a snapshot repository if none exists:
   ```bash
   # Example using a Kubernetes-accessible S3-compatible bucket or NFS mount
   PUT /_snapshot/pre-upgrade-repo
   { "type": "fs", "settings": { "location": "/mnt/snapshots" } }
   ```
2. Take a full cluster snapshot:
   ```bash
   PUT /_snapshot/pre-upgrade-repo/pre-8.17-upgrade
   { "indices": "*", "include_global_state": true }
   ```
3. Wait for snapshot `state: SUCCESS` before proceeding.
   ```bash
   GET /_snapshot/pre-upgrade-repo/pre-8.17-upgrade
   ```

**No snapshot repository is currently configured.** This must be added as a new Kubernetes volume (PVC or shared storage) or an external object-store before the upgrade window.

### 3.2 Shard Allocation Freeze

Disable rebalancing during the rolling restart to prevent unnecessary shard movement:

```bash
PUT /_cluster/settings
{ "persistent": { "cluster.routing.allocation.enable": "primaries" } }
```

Re-enable immediately after the upgrade is complete:

```bash
PUT /_cluster/settings
{ "persistent": { "cluster.routing.allocation.enable": null } }
```

### 3.3 Deprecated Settings Audit

Run the deprecation API on the current cluster and review every CRITICAL-level item:

```bash
GET /_migration/deprecations
```

Known pre-existing items to address (see §4 for remediation):

- `cluster.initial_master_nodes` is set in `elasticsearch.yml` on a fully-formed cluster — this is a deprecation warning in 8.x and an error in 9.x.
- `http.compression: false` (node setting) — evaluate whether compression should be enabled before upgrade.
- `xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate` — consider upgrading to `full` for stricter hostname verification.

### 3.4 Health Verification

```bash
GET /_cluster/health?wait_for_status=green&timeout=60s
GET /_cat/shards?v&h=index,shard,prirep,state,unassigned.reason
```

All shards must be `STARTED` (none `UNASSIGNED`, `INITIALIZING`, or `RELOCATING`) before beginning.

### 3.5 Resource Headroom Check

Current disk usage is ~37% of 5 Gi = ~1.9 Gi per node. During rolling upgrade one node will be unavailable at a time. Elasticsearch will move the downed node's primary shards to replicas on surviving nodes, which requires free space. Confirm:

- Each remaining node has ≥ 10% free disk headroom for temporary shard movement.
- Memory usage per node does not exceed 90% of the 2 Gi container limit before restart.

---

## 4. Required Configuration Changes

These changes must be applied **before** the image bump or they will be included in the new StatefulSet template together with the image update.

### 4.1 Remove `cluster.initial_master_nodes` (REQUIRED)

**Current:** `cluster.initial_master_nodes: es-0,es-1,es-2` in `es-config` ConfigMap.

**Problem:** This setting bootstraps a brand-new cluster. Once the cluster is formed it is ignored at runtime, but it produces a deprecation warning and is an **error** in Elasticsearch 9.x. It must be removed before any 9.x upgrade and should be removed now.

**Change to `es-config` ConfigMap:** delete the `cluster.initial_master_nodes` line from `elasticsearch.yml`.

**Risk:** Low. The cluster is already formed; removing this setting has no effect on a running cluster. A full rolling restart is required to pick up the ConfigMap change (combined with the image bump in §5).

### 4.2 Enable HTTP Compression (RECOMMENDED)

**Current:** `http.compression: false` (set implicitly via the security transport layer — confirmed in node settings API).

**Change:** Add to `elasticsearch.yml`:
```yaml
http.compression: true
```

This reduces response payload sizes for all API clients. There is no correctness impact. Combine with the image bump restart.

### 4.3 Increase JVM Heap (RECOMMENDED)

**Current:** `-Xms512m -Xmx512m` in the `ES_JAVA_OPTS` env var of the StatefulSet.

**Problem:** Elasticsearch best practice is to set heap to 50% of available memory, capped at 31 GB. With a 2 Gi container memory limit, the recommended heap is **1 Gi**. The current 512 Mi leaves the container memory limit severely under-utilized and means more frequent GC under load.

**Change to StatefulSet env:**
```yaml
- name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
  value: "-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
```

Simultaneously, raise the container memory **request** to match the limit to guarantee QoS class `Guaranteed`:
```yaml
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "2Gi"
  limits:
    memory: "2Gi"
```

### 4.4 Transport SSL Verification Mode (RECOMMENDED)

**Current:** `xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate`

This only verifies the certificate is signed by the trusted CA, not that the hostname matches. For a production cluster using pod DNS names as SAN entries in the certificate, upgrade to:

```yaml
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: full
```

**Pre-condition:** Verify that the TLS certificate in `es-http-tls` includes SANs for the pod DNS names (`es-0.es-nodes.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`, etc.). If it does not, regenerate the certificate with proper SANs first, then change the verification mode. Check existing SANs:

```bash
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-http-tls -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' \
  | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -text | grep -A1 "Subject Alternative Name"
```

### 4.5 TLS Certificate Renewal Planning

**Current cert validity:** 2026-06-25 → 2027-06-25 (12 months, self-signed, CA=`es-ca`).

The cert will expire on **2027-06-25**. Plan a renewal at least 30 days before expiry (by **2027-05-26**). The renewal process requires:

1. Re-generate TLS cert/key signed by the same `es-ca` (or rotate the CA as well).
2. Update the `es-http-tls` Secret.
3. Rolling restart of the StatefulSet so pods pick up the new Secret mount.

No action required for this upgrade cycle, but schedule the renewal.

### 4.6 PVC Storage Expansion Planning

**Current:** 5 Gi per node (`standard` StorageClass, RWO).

At 37% usage now (~1.9 Gi), and with `standard` StorageClass supporting online volume expansion (verify with `kubectl get storageclass standard -o yaml | grep allowVolumeExpansion`), plan to expand PVCs before data grows beyond 70% of 5 Gi (~3.5 Gi). Recommended next size: **20 Gi**.

PVC expansion does not require pod restart if the StorageClass supports it, but must be done before the upgrade window if disk headroom is tight.

---

## 5. Rolling Upgrade Procedure

The StatefulSet uses `updateStrategy: RollingUpdate` with `partition: 0`. Elasticsearch requires a rolling upgrade order of **data nodes first, then master-eligible nodes last**. Since all nodes carry all roles in this cluster, the rolling restart order should be from the **highest ordinal down** (es-2 → es-1 → es-0), which matches the StatefulSet default rolling update order.

### Step-by-step

1. **Snapshot** (§3.1) — must be `SUCCESS`.

2. **Disable shard allocation** (§3.2).

3. **Apply ConfigMap changes** (§4.1, §4.2, §4.4):
   ```bash
   kubectl -n elasticsearch edit configmap es-config
   ```

4. **Bump image tag** in the StatefulSet:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n elasticsearch set image statefulset/es \
     elasticsearch=docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.17.X
   ```
   Replace `8.17.X` with the exact patch version after reviewing the release notes.

5. **Monitor rolling restart** — the StatefulSet will restart pods one at a time (es-2, then es-1, then es-0). Wait for each pod to reach `Ready` before the next one starts:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n elasticsearch rollout status statefulset/es --timeout=10m
   ```

6. **Verify cluster health after each pod restart:**
   ```bash
   curl -sk -u elastic:<password> https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cluster/health
   ```
   Ensure `status: green` and `relocating_shards: 0` before the next pod is cycled.

7. **Re-enable shard allocation** (§3.2) after all pods are `Ready` and cluster is green.

8. **Run deprecation API** again on the new version to confirm no remaining CRITICAL items.

9. **Validate `test-index`** is accessible and contains the expected document count.

---

## 6. Post-Upgrade Validation

```bash
# Version check
curl -sk -u elastic:<password> https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/ \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['version']['number'])"

# Cluster health
curl -sk -u elastic:<password> https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cluster/health

# Index integrity
curl -sk -u elastic:<password> https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cat/indices?v

# No unassigned shards
curl -sk -u elastic:<password> \
  "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cat/shards?h=index,shard,prirep,state,unassigned.reason&s=state:desc"

# ILM status
curl -sk -u elastic:<password> https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_ilm/status
```

Expected: version = 8.17.X, health = green, 0 unassigned shards, ILM status = RUNNING.

---

## 7. Rollback Plan

If the upgrade fails on any node and the cluster cannot form a quorum:

1. **Revert the StatefulSet image** to `8.11.1`:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n elasticsearch set image statefulset/es \
     elasticsearch=docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
   ```
2. Rolling restart will restore pods to the previous version. Elasticsearch supports rolling-downgrade within a minor series (8.17 → 8.11 is NOT supported; only 8.17 → 8.16 or adjacent minor). **This means a full snapshot restore from §3.1 may be the only recovery path if a major version mismatch occurs.** Do not skip the snapshot.

3. If a config change (§4.1–§4.4) is the root cause, revert the `es-config` ConfigMap and trigger a rolling restart.

---

## 8. Outstanding Decisions (require SRE lead input)

| # | Item | Options | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Snapshot repository type | NFS PVC, S3-compatible, or GCS bucket | SRE |
| 2 | Exact target patch version | Latest 8.17.X from elastic.co/downloads | SRE |
| 3 | `verification_mode: full` readiness | Check cert SANs; may need cert re-issue first | SRE |
| 4 | PVC expansion timing | Before or after this upgrade? | SRE |
| 5 | Node role specialization | Keep all-role (current) or split master/data | SRE |
| 6 | License upgrade | Basic → Gold/Platinum for cross-cluster, alerting? | Product |

---

## 9. Summary of Changes to Kubernetes Resources

| Resource | Field | From | To |
|---|---|---|---|
| `es-config` ConfigMap | `cluster.initial_master_nodes` | `es-0,es-1,es-2` | **removed** |
| `es-config` ConfigMap | `http.compression` | (absent = false) | `true` |
| `es-config` ConfigMap | `transport.ssl.verification_mode` | `certificate` | `full` (after SAN check) |
| StatefulSet `es` | `image` | `8.11.1` | `8.17.X` |
| StatefulSet `es` | `ES_JAVA_OPTS` | `-Xms512m -Xmx512m` | `-Xms1g -Xmx1g` |
| StatefulSet `es` | `resources.requests.memory` | `1Gi` | `2Gi` |

**No API-level cluster settings changes are required** — `persistent` and `transient` settings are both empty today and will remain so after the upgrade.

**No index changes are required** — `test-index` settings are compatible with 8.17.x without modification.

---

*This document is a planning artifact only. No changes described herein have been applied to the live cluster.*
